title: How Hermes Requisitioned His Groove Back

jokes.text: Finally, the last form of the day. Welcome to stampy town, population five.
jokes.setup: Hermes finishing his work day with forms
jokes.punchline: Treating stamping forms like establishing a town with a ridiculous population count
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Hermes' obsessive bureaucratic personality through creative wordplay about stamping

jokes.text: Sweet gorilla of Manila!
jokes.setup: Hermes needs an exclamation
jokes.punchline: Uses his signature Jamaican-style alliterative exclamation with unexpected animal/location combo
jokes.explanation: Classic Hermes catchphrase that became iconic - perfect character voice with creative alliteration

jokes.text: Attention: You are about to receive a letter from Central Bureaucracy.
jokes.setup: Someone receives a letter
jokes.punchline: The letter announces that you're about to get a letter - peak bureaucratic redundancy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of bureaucratic inefficiency - the meta-announcement perfectly captures the absurdity

jokes.text: I only gamble with chumps. - I'll play. - I'm in!
jokes.setup: Friend says he only gambles with chumps
jokes.punchline: Leela and Fry immediately volunteer, inadvertently calling themselves chumps
jokes.explanation: Classic setup/payoff structure with perfect timing - the immediate responses create the humor

jokes.text: Bah! Big shots. I say let the dice do the talking.
jokes.setup: Context of poker game and fancy friends
jokes.punchline: Bender confuses poker with craps, mixing up 'cards' and 'dice'
jokes.explanation: Captures Bender's confident ignorance - he's trying to sound tough but reveals he doesn't know the game

jokes.text: I got to relax the Jamaican way: warm milk and a good night's sleep.
jokes.setup: Expectation of stereotypical Jamaican relaxation (marijuana reference)
jokes.punchline: Hermes' idea of wild Jamaican relaxation is incredibly wholesome and boring
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character work - subverts cultural stereotypes while showing Hermes' strait-laced personality

jokes.text: Jamaican? I thought you were an outer space potato man.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Hermes being Jamaican
jokes.punchline: Fry's completely nonsensical alternative theory about Hermes' identity
jokes.explanation: Pure Fry stupidity - the specificity of 'outer space potato man' makes it absurdly funny

jokes.text: Hi, Leela. You're fired. - I know.
jokes.setup: Casual greeting followed by firing
jokes.punchline: Leela's matter-of-fact acceptance of being fired mid-conversation
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery - the casual tone contrasts beautifully with the serious content

jokes.text: Office romances never work out. That's how I met my horrible wife.
jokes.setup: Warning against office romances
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself by revealing his own office romance, while calling his wife horrible
jokes.explanation: Good structure - the self-contradiction plus the casual cruelty creates layered humor

jokes.text: She doesn't know I work. I keep my personal life separate.
jokes.setup: Discussion of keeping work and personal life separate
jokes.punchline: Takes it to an absurd extreme - his wife doesn't even know he has a job
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - takes a normal concept to an impossible extreme for absurd humor

jokes.text: I got them off a lucky guy while his lucky seeing-eye dog was whizzing.
jokes.setup: Explaining where he got his 'lucky' glasses
jokes.punchline: Reveals he stole from a blind man while the guide dog was distracted - peak Bender selfishness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender character moment - casually admits to terrible behavior with no shame

jokes.text: Three females, a number and a king giving himself brain surgery.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg describing his poker hand
jokes.punchline: Completely misunderstands playing cards in the most Zoidberg way possible
jokes.explanation: Classic Zoidberg misunderstanding - the specificity of 'king giving himself brain surgery' is perfectly absurd

jokes.text: Read them and leak saltwater.
jokes.setup: 'Read them and weep' poker phrase
jokes.punchline: Bender adapts it for Zoidberg's physiology - crabs leak saltwater instead of crying
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that shows Bender's wit while acknowledging alien physiology

jokes.text: What will I tell my horrible wife? I'd be more worried about the tapeworm going to town on your intestines.
jokes.setup: Friend worried about losing money
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg gives medical advice about a completely unrelated parasite problem
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy - Zoidberg's terrible bedside manner and random medical observations

jokes.text: Oh, my various gods! X-ray specs!
jokes.setup: Discovering Bender's cheating method
jokes.punchline: Hermes' multi-religious exclamation combined with comic book novelty item
jokes.explanation: 'Various gods' is a nice character touch showing Hermes' bureaucratic approach to religion. X-ray specs are appropriately silly.

jokes.text: I swear those are prescription. I need them for reading stuff. Behind other stuff.
jokes.setup: Bender claiming x-ray glasses are prescription
jokes.punchline: Accidentally admits they're for seeing through things while trying to sound innocent
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender logic - tries to lie but accidentally tells the truth in the most incriminating way

jokes.text: No! Don't hurt him! He's my best friend... 's laundry.
jokes.setup: Fry appears to be defending Bender emotionally
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's only worried about his laundry, not Bender
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke - the pause before 'laundry' creates perfect comedic timing

jokes.text: Hey, you wouldn't hit a guy with x-ray glasses, would ya?
jokes.setup: Standard 'wouldn't hit a guy with glasses' plea
jokes.punchline: Bender's version specifies x-ray glasses, making it about the type rather than disability
jokes.explanation: Clever twist on familiar phrase - Bender misses the point about the sympathy appeal

jokes.text: Great cow of Moscow!
jokes.setup: Hermes needs another exclamation
jokes.punchline: Another signature Hermes alliterative exclamation with unexpected animal/location combo
jokes.explanation: Callback to his earlier exclamation style - maintains character consistency with creative variation

jokes.text: Uh, it was ghosts! Big ones! And a tornado!
jokes.setup: Bender needs to explain the destroyed office
jokes.punchline: Escalates from ghosts to big ghosts to adding a tornado for good measure
jokes.explanation: Typical Bender panic lying - the escalation shows his desperation to make the lie more believable

jokes.text: They'll bust me lower than a limbo stick at carnival time! And that's as low as limbo sticks get.
jokes.setup: Hermes worried about being demoted
jokes.punchline: Uses limbo metaphor then immediately over-explains it with bureaucratic precision
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hermes character work - combines his Jamaican background with his obsessive need to clarify everything

jokes.text: Morgan Proctor, grade 19. Hermes Conrad, grade 36.
jokes.setup: Formal bureaucratic introductions
jokes.punchline: The lower grade number is actually higher rank, showing bureaucratic backwards logic
jokes.explanation: Subtle world-building joke about bureaucratic rank systems being counterintuitive

jokes.text: I'm going to jump! - No! - No! Do a flip!
jokes.setup: Hermes threatens suicide, others try to stop him
jokes.punchline: Bender encourages it but requests entertainment value
jokes.explanation: Peak Bender callousness - perfectly timed dark humor that became iconic and quotable

jokes.text: Old friend, don't jump! Use a method that won't damage your liver. Others need it, you know?
jokes.setup: Zoidberg appears to be giving caring advice about suicide
jokes.punchline: He's only concerned about organ harvesting potential
jokes.explanation: Dark but perfectly in character - Zoidberg's medical background makes this twisted logic work

jokes.text: I'm only anal 78.36% of the time.
jokes.setup: Hermes claims he's not cut out to be a bureaucrat
jokes.punchline: Proves his anal nature by giving an extremely precise percentage
jokes.explanation: Brilliant self-defeating argument - Hermes undermines his own point through the very behavior he's denying

jokes.text: Sweet something of... Of someplace.
jokes.setup: Hermes needs another exclamation
jokes.punchline: He's so defeated he can't even complete his signature exclamation pattern
jokes.explanation: Excellent character work - his depression breaks down even his most consistent speech pattern

jokes.text: Oh! The ultimate penalty.
jokes.setup: Hermes is told he gets paid vacation
jokes.punchline: A bureaucrat considers paid time off to be the worst possible punishment
jokes.explanation: Perfect character insight - only Hermes would see vacation as punishment because he loves work

jokes.text: ...they give me krill for everyone I send.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg recommending a health spa
jokes.punchline: Reveals he gets kickbacks in the form of his preferred food
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Zoidberg's corruption is perfectly suited to his species' diet

jokes.text: I assign me. I accept. Welcome.
jokes.setup: Morgan needs to assign someone to fill in for Hermes
jokes.punchline: Has a full bureaucratic conversation with herself in three quick sentences
jokes.explanation: Efficient bureaucratic absurdity - shows how formal process continues even in ridiculous circumstances

jokes.text: Fry got his head stuck in a crater. - I thought it'd fit.
jokes.setup: Explanation of why a delivery took too long
jokes.punchline: Fry's reasoning for attempting something clearly impossible
jokes.explanation: Classic Fry stupidity - the matter-of-fact delivery makes his bad reasoning funnier

jokes.text: I'd have won but I ran out of olives.
jokes.setup: Bender explaining losing a drinking contest with the autopilot
jokes.punchline: Blames losing on running out of garnish rather than alcohol tolerance
jokes.explanation: Good Bender logic - focuses on the wrong aspect entirely, showing his priorities

jokes.text: Why isn't this alphabetical? - What? - The zipper should be at the bottom.
jokes.setup: Morgan critiquing Fry's locker organization
jokes.punchline: Her idea of alphabetical order includes putting zippers at the bottom
jokes.explanation: Brilliant bureaucratic thinking - applying alphabetical logic to physical objects in the most literal way

jokes.text: It used to be milk, and time makes fools of us all.
jokes.setup: Morgan asking why there's yogurt in his locker
jokes.punchline: Fry gives unexpectedly profound explanation for spoiled milk
jokes.explanation: Unexpectedly poetic for Fry - the philosophical depth applied to spoiled dairy is genuinely clever

jokes.text: This is the other Fry's locker. I'm 'Phry.'
jokes.setup: Fry trying to escape blame for his messy locker
jokes.punchline: Creates an alter ego with a ridiculous spelling variation
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay that fits Fry's simple-minded approach to problem solving

jokes.text: Dirty boy! Dirty, dirty, dirty!
jokes.setup: Morgan appears to be scolding Fry professionally
jokes.punchline: Her tone reveals unexpected sexual excitement about his messiness
jokes.explanation: Good character twist - subverts expectations about the strict bureaucrat's true desires

jokes.text: I'm surrounded by neat all day. Nothing's kinkier than a slop-jock.
jokes.setup: Morgan explaining her attraction to messiness
jokes.punchline: 'Slop-jock' as a term of endearment for messy people
jokes.explanation: Creative term and good character psychology - the repressed bureaucrat's rebellion makes sense

jokes.text: Like every girl I dated.
jokes.setup: Morgan saying she'll deny their relationship
jokes.punchline: Fry notes this is his standard dating experience
jokes.explanation: Standard self-deprecating humor that fits Fry's romantic history

jokes.text: This is no spa, it's a labor camp.
jokes.setup: Hermes was sent to a relaxing health spa
jokes.punchline: Discovers it's actually forced labor disguised as wellness
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the spa being fake - classic bait and switch

jokes.text: Don't know why I go to him.
jokes.setup: Asking to give Zoidberg a message of revenge
jokes.punchline: Admits he doesn't know why he uses Zoidberg as a doctor
jokes.explanation: Running gag about Zoidberg's incompetence - everyone knows he's bad but uses him anyway

jokes.text: Under who? - The autopilot. - That drunk?
jokes.setup: Leela being demoted to copilot
jokes.punchline: Reminds us the autopilot has a drinking problem
jokes.explanation: Good callback that reinforces the absurdity of the ship's hierarchy

jokes.text: It's full-time.
jokes.setup: Bender assigned to handle Professor's bodily functions
jokes.punchline: Clarifies it's a full-time job, implying the Professor is very needy
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - makes the Professor's neediness funnier by emphasizing the time commitment

jokes.text: I threw the professor at her. I had to hit her with a purse I found.
jokes.setup: Bender explaining a confrontation with a woman in the park
jokes.punchline: His solution escalates from throwing an old man to hitting with a random purse
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Bender's violent problem-solving and complete disregard for the Professor's wellbeing

jokes.text: Morgan? It's 3 a.m. A skunk knocked over my garbage, and I could only think of you.
jokes.setup: Fry calling Morgan late at night
jokes.punchline: His idea of a romantic gesture is comparing her to garbage-related chaos
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry romantic gesture - completely misunderstands what's appealing but fits their dynamic

jokes.text: I ordered him to flush out the professor's earwax.
jokes.setup: Explaining where Bender is
jokes.punchline: Reveals Fry is now giving orders about gross medical procedures
jokes.explanation: Shows Fry's new authority through disgusting tasks - fits the show's gross-out humor

jokes.text: Hey, Fry? I made you a candle with... What the...?
jokes.setup: Bender bringing Fry a gift
jokes.punchline: Walks in on Fry and Morgan together
jokes.explanation: Standard 'walking in on' setup - functional story moment but not particularly funny

jokes.text: The promotion, the dwarf in my book club who steals my opinions.
jokes.setup: Bender realizing the connection between events
jokes.punchline: Includes a completely unrelated complaint about his book club
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender non-sequitur - the specificity of 'dwarf who steals my opinions' is perfect

jokes.text: Come back! It's a loop!
jokes.setup: Bender chasing Fry and Morgan
jokes.punchline: Warns them about running in what appears to be a circular hallway
jokes.explanation: Visual gag about futuristic architecture - Bender's practical warning amid the chase

jokes.text: You didn't understand? I'll explain. You were having sex with you.
jokes.setup: Fry saying Morgan didn't understand something
jokes.punchline: Bender's blunt, confusing explanation of what he witnessed
jokes.explanation: Bender's straightforward approach to awkward situations - the grammar confusion adds to it

jokes.text: That's what makes it so juicy. It's the forbidden fruit angle.
jokes.setup: Morgan saying she can't fraternize due to her rank
jokes.punchline: Bender sees the rule-breaking as adding excitement
jokes.explanation: Standard Bender encouragement of rule-breaking - the 'forbidden fruit' reference is apt

jokes.text: I am Bender. Please insert girder.
jokes.setup: Bender's brain has been removed
jokes.punchline: His body defaults to basic construction robot functions
jokes.explanation: Good concept - shows what Bender is underneath his personality, just a basic construction robot

jokes.text: But... But Bender need brain. For smart making!
jokes.setup: Fry protesting Bender's brain being filed away
jokes.punchline: Uses caveman grammar while arguing for intelligence
jokes.explanation: Good irony - Fry's argument for brain importance is undermined by his own grammar

jokes.text: Without his brain, he's quiet and helpful.
jokes.setup: Observing brainless Bender
jokes.punchline: Points out that losing his brain actually improved Bender's personality
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about Bender's character - suggests his personality is more annoying than his absence

jokes.text: A friend went mad trying to find their washroom.
jokes.setup: Warning about Central Bureaucracy's complexity
jokes.punchline: Someone had a mental breakdown just looking for a bathroom
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how byzantine bureaucracy can be - finding a bathroom becomes impossible

jokes.text: It's inefficient! Pulling empty carts is the closest we get to sleep.
jokes.setup: Hermes observing the labor camp's cart system
jokes.punchline: Even as a prisoner, he can't help but optimize workflow, then reveals how tired they are
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hermes character moment - even in prison his bureaucratic mind focuses on efficiency

jokes.text: You know, you ought to be a bureaucrat.
jokes.setup: Hermes suggesting efficiency improvements
jokes.punchline: Not realizing he already is one
jokes.explanation: Good irony that the guard doesn't recognize Hermes' obvious bureaucratic nature. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: Someone had a baby.
jokes.setup: Long line at Central Bureaucracy
jokes.punchline: Treating childbirth as just another bureaucratic event requiring paperwork
jokes.explanation: Good bureaucratic humor - reduces human experience to paperwork requirements

jokes.text: I am Bender. Please insert girder.
jokes.setup: Trying to sneak Bender into Central Bureaucracy
jokes.punchline: His repeated catchphrase while brainless
jokes.explanation: Callback that reinforces the running gag of brainless Bender's limited vocabulary

jokes.text: Damn it, jump already! Stop hogging the healthy liver!
jokes.setup: Zoidberg encouraging someone to commit suicide
jokes.punchline: Reveals his continued interest in organ harvesting
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Zoidberg's earlier organ obsession - maintains his disturbing medical priorities

jokes.text: ...is done by a single Australian man.
jokes.setup: Hermes explaining how he organized the labor spa
jokes.punchline: All physical labor is now done by one extremely overworked Australian
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist escalation - the specificity of 'Australian' makes it funnier than just 'one man'

jokes.text: We kept it gray.
jokes.setup: Boasting about being on a committee to review a regulation book's color
jokes.punchline: After all that work, they chose the most boring possible color
jokes.explanation: Perfect bureaucratic absurdity - endless committee work for the most mundane possible outcome

jokes.text: I'm Bender, baby! Please insert liquor!
jokes.setup: Bender gets his brain back
jokes.punchline: Immediately returns to form with his signature request, but for alcohol instead of girders
jokes.explanation: Perfect return to form - shows Bender's personality is fully restored with his signature phrase modified for his priorities

jokes.text: Good bureaucrats never finish early.
jokes.setup: Hermes finished the task with time to spare
jokes.punchline: In bureaucracy, efficiency is actually punished
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of bureaucratic inefficiency - perfectly captures how red tape rewards slowness

jokes.text: A date that ended in disappointment.
jokes.setup: Morgan's prom date being mentioned
jokes.punchline: Her matter-of-fact assessment of what was presumably a traumatic teenage experience
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Morgan's clinical approach to personal experiences

jokes.text: No! No! I was young and reckless!
jokes.setup: Morgan caught in a bureaucratic error
jokes.punchline: For a strict bureaucrat, only stamping something 4 times instead of 5 was 'reckless'
jokes.explanation: Perfect character comedy - Morgan's idea of wild youthful rebellion is a minor paperwork error

jokes.text: You are technically correct. The best kind of correct.
jokes.setup: Hermes finding a technical violation in Morgan's paperwork
jokes.punchline: Celebrates technical correctness as the highest form of being right
jokes.explanation: Became an iconic internet meme - perfectly captures bureaucratic mentality while being genuinely quotable

jokes.text: Come on, wife! Let's blow this joint!
jokes.setup: Hermes getting his job back
jokes.punchline: Calls his wife 'wife' rather than her name, callback to the poker friend's horrible wife
jokes.explanation: Subtle callback to the episode's earlier marriage jokes while showing Hermes' happiness

tmdb_overview: When Hermes takes a stress-relieving vacation, replacement bureaucrat Morgan Proctor becomes infatuated with Fry. Bender threatens to publicize their affair, but Morgan removes Bender's memory and hides it within the cavernous Central Bureaucracy.
